scope.summary: Implement MDD (Lightwave/Maya point-cache) and PC2 (3ds Max/Houdini geometry-cache) binary export of simulated garment frame sequences as fast lightweight alternatives to OBJ sequence for DCC pipelines that support these formats natively
acceptance_criteria: POST /tailor/garments/:id/export with format=mdd writes a valid MDD binary file (4-byte-LE magic, frame count, point count, then packed f32 XYZ per point per frame) to the export artifact directory
POST /tailor/garments/:id/export with format=pc2 writes a valid PC2 binary file (12-byte header: magic + version + numPoints + startFrame + sampleRate + numSamples, then packed XYZ f32 frames)
Both formats read back correctly in Blender (verify by scripted Blender headless test included in the integration test suite)
Export emits TailorGarmentExportCompleted with export_format='mdd' or 'pc2'
No external crate dependency required: MDD and PC2 are trivial binary formats implementable with std::io
scope.risk_if_missed: Maya and Houdini cloth pipelines consume MDD/PC2 natively at far higher performance than OBJ sequence (binary vs text). Without these formats, Tailor is excluded from professional VFX studios whose DCC pipelines are built around Maya geometry-cache workflows.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
